releases.title: v2.10.11
releases.summary: This patch release tones down the app’s remaining heavy overlay shells so the camera modal and the main slide-in drawers feel calmer and more functional. The overlays still focus attention and behave the same way, but they no longer rely as heavily on blur, oversized rounding, or dramatic shell/poster shadowing to create that sense of separation.

releases.title: v2.10.10
releases.summary: This patch release makes the add/edit drawers feel more like normal working surfaces and less like staged modal moments. The editor header keeps the same structure and actions, but the title no longer shouts in forced all-caps display styling and instead reads like a calmer product workspace heading.

releases.title: v2.10.9
releases.summary: This patch release removes the remaining “control plane” language from the mobile shell so the app header reads like a normal product instead of a product-marketing or ops-console surface. Mobile admin and support context is still visible, but the wording is now plainer and more honest about what the user is actually looking at.

releases.title: v2.10.8
releases.summary: This patch release tones down the library shell navigation so it keeps its hierarchy and space context without feeling over-styled. The sidebar no longer leans on uppercase eyebrows, badge-like role chrome, gold active rails, or decorative sub-item bullets to explain itself, and instead reads more like ordinary product navigation.

releases.title: v2.10.7
releases.summary: This patch release finishes the shared tab-strip cleanup so the app’s remaining secondary tab and mode-toggle surfaces now use the same flattened tab language as the newer import, admin, help, and drawer flows. The old pill-shell tab strip is gone from the shared frontend layer, and auth, space management, library, events, and collectibles now all rely on the same shared tab primitive instead of preserving one-off segmented-control styling.

releases.title: v2.10.6
releases.summary: This patch release packages the post-`2.10.0` UI and workflow follow-through that was completed across the library and add/edit drawers. Import now reads like the rest of the product, bulk selection is calmer and more usable, the shell has less decorative chrome, and Add/Edit search now behaves like a true universal lookup flow instead of a barcode-only side path. The result is a quieter, more predictable product surface without changing the underlying multi-format ownership model that shipped in `2.10.0`.

releases.title: v2.10.0
releases.summary: This release upgrades the library from a single-format ownership model to a true multi-format ownership model. A single title can now carry more than one owned format without fragmenting into duplicate library records, and the main library add/edit flows, import flows, and format filtering all understand that ownership shape. Backward compatibility is preserved through a derived primary `format` value so the existing library surfaces can keep functioning while `owned_formats` becomes the canonical storage model.

releases.title: v2.9.9
releases.summary: This release moves External Logs from an env-only operator setup toward a real admin-managed control plane. Admin can now configure the common external log endpoint fields inside `Admin -> Integrations -> External Logs`, validate the running target, review the latest validation result, and understand whether the current runtime is using saved settings, env defaults, or an immutable env override. The existing fail-safe behavior remains intact: external export stays additive, collector failure stays non-blocking, and the primary DB-backed audit path remains durable.

releases.title: v2.9.8
releases.summary: This release ships two connected slices of hardening work. First, the app’s add/edit drawers, Help surfaces, and tabbed workflows were normalized onto calmer shared UI primitives so the product reads more consistently and accessibly across media, events, collectibles, help, and admin flows. Second, the runtime observability story was hardened for longer-lived operator use with clearer diagnostics, supported collector-path smokes, persistence rehearsals, and a repeatable observability release-evidence artifact that proves logs and metrics stay recoverable without turning collectors into a hidden runtime dependency.

releases.title: v2.9.6
releases.summary: This release introduces the first explicit `platform` versus `homelab` product-edition boundary inside the private repo. The backend now owns the active edition contract, the frontend shell and Help surface follow that server-owned shape, and platform-only control-plane routes no longer leak into homelab. The release also adds repeatable runtime smoke gates and CI enforcement so both sides of the split stay proven as the repo moves toward a later product/repo separation.

details.heading: Product edition boundary
bullets: Added a backend-owned edition contract with supported values:
Exposed the active `product_edition` through auth bootstrap so the frontend reads server-owned edition state instead of client-only assumptions.
Added a frontend shell composition boundary for homelab so platform control-plane surfaces are excluded as mounted routes and nav affordances, not just hidden late in rendering.

details.heading: Homelab shell and Help surface
bullets: Homelab Help now mounts a shared Help experience with:
Homelab shell/nav no longer exposes platform control-plane affordances such as:
Direct homelab navigation to platform-only dashboard tabs is redirected to homelab-safe destinations.

details.heading: Backend route-group separation
bullets: Split backend route mounting into shared versus platform-only groups so homelab no longer mounts platform-only APIs such as:
Kept shared homelab-safe surfaces mounted, including:

details.heading: Homelab product-shape tightening
bullets: Homelab shared auth/scope/library responses now keep:
Homelab registration is now invite-free after the first admin, removing the remaining dependency on the platform tenant-invite model for local account creation.
Homelab rejects generic authenticated space switching through shared scope APIs.
Homelab still allows meaningful library creation and library selection inside the single-context product shape.

details.heading: Runtime proof and CI enforcement
bullets: Added a repeatable homelab edition-boundary smoke gate to verify the shared mounted surfaces and unmounted platform-only APIs against a live homelab stack.
Added a matching platform edition-boundary smoke gate to verify that invite-based registration and the tenant/admin control plane remain mounted in platform.
Wired both edition gates into the blocking CI/release workflow so publish and preflight paths now depend on both sides of the product split.
